event_description: An airborne camera surveys an airport apron featuring an aircraft with an unconventional design: a twin-boom 'pusher' configuration (where the engine/propeller is at the rear of the main fuselage pod). The EO view clearly shows this unique structural layout. The IR view confirms the operational status by revealing a distinct, intense thermal hotspot located at the rear of the central pod, defying standard single-engine airplane expectations.

qa.question: In the early EO segment, which cue pairing is visible for the object?

options.A: the object with rear engine hotspot tied to pusher geometry; standard nose-engine expectations nearby
options.B: the object with standard nose-engine expectations; rear engine hotspot tied to pusher geometry nearby
options.C: the object with apron, stand, grass, or pavement geometry; runway, taxiway, or apron markings nearby
options.D: the object with runway, taxiway, or apron markings; camera orbit or scale change nearby

qa.evidence_note: the twin-boom structure places the engine/propeller at the rear of the fuselage pod

qa.question: Across the EO scale or angle change, which fixed-state cue stays paired with the object?

options.A: standard nose-engine expectations; view scale or angle changes
options.B: apron, stand, grass, or pavement geometry; view scale or angle changes
options.C: runway, taxiway, or apron markings; view scale or angle changes
options.D: rear engine hotspot tied to pusher geometry; view scale or angle changes

qa.evidence_note: the twin-boom structure places the engine/propeller at the rear of the fuselage pod

options.B: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast; standard nose-engine expectations
options.C: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast; rear engine hotspot tied to pusher geometry
options.D: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast; runway, taxiway, or apron markings

qa.evidence_note: an intense hotspot appears at the rear of the central pod

options.A: runway, taxiway, or apron markings; near the object
options.B: rear engine hotspot tied to pusher geometry; near the object

group_scoring_recommendation.C1: Use squared-ratio consistency scoring for the eight-step parallel cue chain.
group_scoring_recommendation.H1: Prefer prefix scoring because the four questions form a cue-chain verdict.

notes.annotation_visibility_warning: Do not export main_event, event_description, answer, option_roles, evidence_note, rationales, or time_reference metadata to model prompts.
notes.design_revision_note: Video-reasoning v15 text-gate repair r2: options are neutral cue-pair choices with same-scene distractors, reducing answerability from wording alone.
acceptance_gates: Run request key-path leakage audit after export.
Run Qwen text-only/no-video baseline before video inference.
Reject samples or templates whose text-only accuracy suggests wording shortcuts.
Keep evidence_note fields internal; they are for annotation audit only.
